entry.title: Re: Is there a way to apply a selection from a filter in...

It really depends on you use case and how would user interact with input control 2. 

For example, if value taken from input control 1 will be just used as default value of input control 2?  

Enable cascading between two input controls can already partially achieve the basic requirement. 

Bottomline, you can always achieve this by scripting in story.

Only FG is valuating with Sale order as per sales quantity. Please give solution for this issue.

entry.title: SAP Enable Now 2406 Release Overview
entry.content:
Introduction

It’s now been just over a month since the 2406 release of SAP Enable Now was deployed into Production and I can genuinely say that I still consider the release fondly.  The release includes many great enhancements, including several that have the potential to significantly improve the efficiency and productivity of SAP Enable Now content authors and users.  It also includes enhancements that extend far beyond the use of SAP Enable Now, enhancements that have the potential to increase the adoption of all SAP systems.  I encourage everyone interested in SAP Enable Now to explore these new features and take advantage of the enhanced capabilities.

The rest of this post focusses on the key enhancements included in the release.

2406 Release Notes

The 2406 release notes are available from https://help.sap.com/.  After accessing the site, log in using your SAP Universal ID, and navigate to the What’s New area on the SAP Enable Now site.  Many of the enhancement descriptions in the release notes contain links to useful resources, e.g. Simulations/tutorials.  Be sure to check out these resources when you go through the notes.

If you are interested in learning more about some of the key enhancement included in the 2406 release, watch this recording of a 2406 Release Overview session.  It is also worth going through the New in 2406 content in Info Center.

My Top 5 Enhancements

Whilst there are many great enhancements in the 2406 release, my top 5 enhancements for the release are:

  1. Deploying customer-specific content through SAP Companion for SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) - With this enhancement, it is now possible to create and deploy customer-specific SAP Companion content for SAC.  This feature ensures that users of SAC can easily access useful information as they work in the platform, enabling them to continuously learn and improve their skills as they work.

    If you are interested in using custom content in SAP Companion, refer to the webinar recording from a recent session on this topic.

  2. Using SAP Companion with SAP GUI for HTML and Web Dynpro apps – It is now possible to use SAP Companion with hybrid SAP applications that include a combination of Fiori, GUI for HTML and Web Dynpro apps. This feature allows customers using these hybrid technologies to deploy SAP Companion for their entire end to end processes, improving productivity and adoption of those applications.

    For more information about using SAP Companion with SAP GUI for HTML and Web Dynpro apps, refer to the Features and Configuration > Help for SAP GUI for HTML and Web Dynpro section of the SAP Companion Integration Guide or to the What’s New in SAP S/4HANA 2023 guide.  (Don’t forget, you must log into https://help.sap.com/, using your SAP Universal ID, before you can access content on this site.)

  3. Managing Business Role information in Enable Now - In the previous release of SAP Enable Now (2311), Business Role functionality was introduced that allows content authors to limit access to content objects to specific user groups.  In the initial release, content authors had to manually created Business Roles in Manager, from corresponding User Roles; this could be time consuming. 

    With this release, Business Roles details can now be imported into Enable Now Manager from a customer’s Identity Provider system, through the SCIM API.  This significantly reduces the manual effort associated with creating and maintaining Business Roles in Enable Now Manager.

    Whilst the majority of the configuration of the SCIM interface is performed in the Identity Provider system, there are some instructions on the use of this interface with Enable Now in the Manager (HANA) Operations Guide.

  4. Generating eLearning content - This release enables content authors to automatically generate eLearning content, such as business process descriptions, using Generative AI (genAI) capability. This will significantly reduce the time and effort associated with authoring new content and allow authors to create useful content on topics they may not fully understand.

    The full genAI capability is now in Enable Now Production, however, it is currently necessary to request that this capability be activated in your SAP Enable Now environment.  Hopefully this will only be necessary for a short period of time.  The instructions for activating the genAI capability in your Enable Now environment are available from this SAP Note.

  5. Sharing information between SAP Enable Now and SAP Cloud ALM - SAP Enable Now is now integrated with SAP Cloud ALM, allowing ALM business process details to be shared with Enable Now users and Enable Now content, such as Simulations, to be shared with ALM users. This integration reduces the time and effort required to provide both user groups with relevant information.  When it comes to Cloud systems, these types of integrations significantly reduce the time and effort to deploy changes, which are constant, so an important part of every implementation methodology.

    Instructions for integrating SAP Enable Now and Cloud ALM are available from the SAP Cloud ALM Integration Guide.

Other Significant Improvements

Outside my top 5, which I’m sure that you will agree are wonderful enhancements, there were a significant number of other great improvements introduced in the 2406 release.  In general, many of these enhancements can be categorized into two areas:

Web-based authoring enhancements

Unless you are new to SAP Enable Now, I’m sure that you will have noticed that many improvements have been made to the Manager content authoring capability over the last two years.  These changes have the potential to simplify the authoring process for ‘part-time’ content authors, such as Subject Matter Experts, and also enable content authoring on Macs.

In the 2406 release, the enhancements are focussed on improving the web-based recording capability.  It is now possible, using the new capability, to add audio to Enable Now Simulations, during the recording process, and to record the content author, rather than the active application on your PC screen.  This capability allows content authors to record snippets of video of themselves, potentially introducing a topic or discussing a non-system topic, such as changes to business processes.  Both of these enhancements should result in higher quality, more flexible eLearning content so are welcome improvements.

If you are interested in learning about some of the recent enhancements to web-based authoring in Manager, the section on Web Authoring in the Info Center is a great place to start.

And finally on this topic, if you are wondering whether the enhancements to web-based authoring are a fad or a sign of things to come, take a look at the Enable Now Roadmap.  There are more of these changes in the pipeline so I’d spend some time on this topic if you haven’t already.

SAP Companion enhancements

The second major category of enhancement that occurred during Q2 2024, was continual improvements to SAP Companion.  I mentioned the release of the improved integration with SAC and the support for GUI for HTML and Web Dynpro apps earlier in the post, which are both significant enhancements in their own right, but there were others.  These included the ability to control access to SAP Companion content by date and improvements to the stability of content in SuccessFactors, which makes it easier to assign SAP Companion content to the objects on SuccessFactors screens.  In short, SAP Companion now works better with SuccessFactors.

The last SAP Companion change that I’d like to highlight from the last quarter is centered Callouts.  This functionalilty provides yet another means for SAP Companion customers to push critical information to their application users, as they use the corresponding functionality, so that they can be kept informed of changes to that functionality.  Sounds like something that would be useful to every SAP customer, particularly those with Cloud applications, if you ask me.

Further information about SAP Companion enhancements can be accessed through the SAP Companion Release Notes.

Summary

So, there you have it, another wonderful release from the SAP Enable Now and SAP Companion product teams.  If that doesn’t excite you, you’re a tough audience.

My final advice to anyone that is interested in SAP Enable Now and SAP Companion is to spend some time keeping abreast of the changes.  SAP Enable Now and SAP Companion are both maturing quickly so if you blink, you might miss the best thing since sliced bread. 

Remember, our goal is the same as your goal; happy productive SAP application users.  I look forward to hearing about how you’ve used some of the new capability to make the life of your SAP users better!

entry.title: SAP CPI call oData v2 service defined in SAP CAP project
entry.content:
I am developing a SAP CAP project, which includes an entity S4_DATA.

 

entity S4_DATA {
    key ID              : UUID;
        EXIDV           : String(20);
        PARENT_DETAILS  : Association to many S4_PARENTS
                              on PARENT_DETAILS.EXIDV = $self.EXIDV;
}

entity S4_PARENTS {
    key LABELID         : String(50);
    key SERIALNO        : Integer;
    key ITEMNO          : String(6);
        EXIDV           : String(20);
        MATNR           : String(40);
        CHARG           : String(10);
        PARENT_LABELID  : String(50);
        PARENT_SERIALNO : Integer;
}

 

I expose it as OData service as per below:

 

using mynamespace from '../db/mynamespace';
service CatalogService {
   entity S4_DATA as projection on mynamespace.S4_DATA;
   entity S4_PARENTS as projection on mynamespace.S4_PARENTS;
}

 

In s4_srv.js file, I have below codes to use OData v2 adapter:

 

cds.on('bootstrap', app => app.use(odataV2()));

 

In same file, I also have an event handler for OData 'CREATE' operation on 'S4_DATA':

 

srv.before('CREATE', 'S4_DATA', async (req) => {
  process.stderr.write(`Request headers: ${JSON.stringify(req.headers)}\n`);
  process.stderr.write(`content-type: ${req.headers['content-type']}\n`); 
  if (req.data) {
    process.stderr.write(`Request data: ${JSON.stringify(req.data, null, 2)}\n`);
  }
});

 

To locally simulate the call from SAP CPI, I use below test script:

 

### Mock SAP CPI Call Data01
POST http://localhost:4004/odata/v2/catalog/S4_DATA
Content-Type: application/xml

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<entry>
    <content>
        <properties>
            <EXIDV>10000000000000800668</EXIDV>
            <PARENT_DETAILS>
                <ITEM>
                    <ITEMNO>000001</ITEMNO>
                    <EXIDV>10000000001000000948</EXIDV>
                    <MATNR>PGK-001</MATNR>
                    <CHARG>ABCD-44</CHARG>
                </ITEM>
                <ITEM>
                    <ITEMNO>000002</ITEMNO>
                    <EXIDV>10000000001000000949</EXIDV>
                    <MATNR>PGK-002</MATNR>
                    <CHARG>ABCD-55</CHARG>
                </ITEM>
            </PARENT_DETAILS>
        </properties>
    </content>
</entry>

 

The output from the test script are as follow:

 

[odata] - POST /odata/v4/catalog/S4_DATA
Request headers: {"connection":"close","host":"localhost:4004","content-length":"371","accept-encoding":"gzip, deflate","content-type":"application/json;IEEE754Compatible=true","user-agent":"vscode-restclient","x-cds-odata-version":"v2","x-correlation-id":"db52783b-5fc8-4a6e-9dc3-ac084d93269b"}
content-type: application/json;IEEE754Compatible=true
Request data: {
  "EXIDV": "10000000000000800668",
  "ID": "06d14828-174b-4d07-be83-6defbe3a4a01"
}

 

From the output, I notice 2 things:
1. 'content-type' is "application/json" even though I set it as 'application/xml' in the test script.
2. The 'PARENT_DETAILS' is missing from 'Request data': "req.data".

After some study I realize that the transformation ("xml" => "json") is likely happening at the OData adapter level in SAP CAP.
I try to use below codes to intercepts all incoming requests before they reach the OData adapter, but after testing, I found that it is never triggered.

 

    cds.on('bootstrap', app => {
        app.use((req, res, next) => {
            process.stderr.write('Request received\n');
            next();
        });
        app.use(odataV2());
    });

 

Has anyone else experienced this issue in the past? Your feedback and suggestions would be highly valued.

Notes:
Below are some screen shot in SAP CPI, when CPI call OData service, it only allow xml as its payload instead of JSON.

entry.title: Task 9 - Using CQL in an unbound action (July Developer Challenge - "Reverse APIs")
entry.content:
This is a task in the July Developer Challenge - "Reverse APIs".

In this task you'll define and implement an unbound action, that does something that you might remember from the Developer Challenge on APIs last year in August, and you'll end up writing some (hopefully) interesting code for the implementation.

Background

Last year we ran a Developer Challenge on APIs. In one of the tasks - Task 3 - Have a Northbreeze product selected for you - you needed to make a call to an API endpoint supplying your SAP Community ID and it would return a Northwind product. In the example for my ID, "qmacro", it showed this being returned:

{
  "@odata.context": "$metadata#Edm.String",
  "value": "Rössle Sauerkraut"
}
In other words, the product "corresponding to" my SAP Community ID is "Rössle Sauerkraut". Different products were returned for different SAP Community IDs, so that your answers would be different to each other. The calculation of which product to return was based on the value of your SAP Community ID.

In this task, you will need to define an API endpoint, in the form of an unbound action, and write the implementation to calculate, look up and return the name of a product that corresponds to the SAP Community ID that is sent by the TESTER.

Correspondence calculation

So how should you determine which product to select for a given SAP Community ID? The same way that it was determined in last year's Developer Challenge for that task. And that is to turn the SAP Community ID string value into a numeric value, and then use that to select a specific product with an ID (in ProductID) that matches that numeric value.

Here's the logic:

First:

  • ensure the SAP Community ID is all lower case
  • convert each character to its equivalent decimal ASCII code
  • add the resulting list of ASCII codes together to make a total

Then:

  • determine how many products are in your Northbreeze data
  • read a single product where the key is the ASCII codes total value

Noting that:

  • you will need to use modulo arithmetic to ensure that the ASCII codes total value falls within the range between 1 and the total number of Northbreeze products

And here's an example of that logic in action, where (for the sake of illustration) the SAP Community ID value "QmacrO" is sent in the payload of the HTTP request:

  • converting "QmacrO" to lower case gives "qmacro"
  • the decimal ASCII codes for "qmacro" are 113, 109, 97, 99, 114 and 111
  • added together, these codes come to 643
  • there are 77 Northbreeze products
  • using modulo arithmetic to turn 643 into a number between 1 and 77, it becomes 28
  • the product with ID 28 is Rössle Sauerkraut

Be careful with the modulo calculation, because you need to take into account that the result of a modulo calculation could be 0, which is not in the ID range. It would also never be 77. This is why 643 modulo 77 becomes 28, not 27 (basically, do the modulo calculation and add 1).

Clearly these instructions have already told you that there are 77 products. You should have 77 products too if you started with the repo as recommended. Nevertheless, we encourage you to use some CQL in your implementation to programatically determine how many products there are, not just set the value 77 in a constant 🙂

The requirements

Here are the specific requirements for this task.

Add an unbound function called selectProduct to the definition of your northbreeze service. It should expect a String value for a single parameter named communityid, and it should return the same type as the ProductName element in the Products entity type.

Note that although you will of course be supplying your SAP Community ID as normal when you submit to the TESTER (see the next section), as usual, it will be the TESTER that picks and sends an SAP Community ID to your endpoint, not you, of course.

In the implementation, use CQL to determine the number of products you have, and then use the logic described in the "Correspondence calculation" section earlier to determine the product ID, based on the (semi-random) SAP Community ID value received. Then use CQL to retrieve that product and return the product name.

Submitting to the TESTER

Now you're ready to submit your CANDIDATE service, with this new API endpoint, to the TESTER!

The payload

The task identifier you need to supply in the payload of your submission is: northbreeze-selectProduct.

You'll have already done this sort of thing previously so just head back there for the more detailed instructions if you need them, or to the the section titled "The Tester service, and making a test request" in the main challenge blog post.

You'll need to submit a JSON payload like this:

{
  "communityid": "<your-community-id>",
  "serviceurl": "<the-URL-of-your-service>",
  "task": "northbreeze-selectProduct"
}
And, just as with the previous (and all further tasks):

  • the value for the communityid property should be your ID on this SAP Community platform (e.g. mine is "qmacro")

  • the value for the serviceurl property should be the absolute URL (i.e. including the scheme), of your CANDIDATE service which contains the API endpoint (see ℹ️ A note on URLs and services).

That's it!

Logging of test results

Remember that you can check on your progress, and the progress of your fellow participants - all requests are logged and are available in an entity set served by the TESTER service. The entity set URL is https://developer-challenge-2024-07.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/tester/Testlog and being an OData V4 entity set, all the normal OData system query options are available to you for digging into that information.

Until the next task, have fun, and if you have any questions or comments, leave them below!

entry.title: Introducing SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint
entry.content:
Great News! With the latest releases of 2024.14 for the SAP Analytics Cloud fast track customers (BI weekly release model) and for Quarterly release cycle with 2024 Q3 QRC, SAP Analytics Cloud, Add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint is now Generally available.

https://appsource.microsoft.com/en-us/product/office/WA200007270?tab=Overview 

 

 

1. What is SAP Analytics Cloud, Add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint?

Microsoft Office tools like PowerPoint and Excel are widely used tools. Integration and Embedding of SAC chart widgets into your business application will help customers to make confident decisions without switching the context.

Launching the New SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint, You can now embed the SAP Analytics Cloud story widgets into PowerPoint as an Image which can be dynamically updated with the latest Data.

Business Value:

  1. Streamline Presentation Updates: Instead of manually copying and pasting Widgets from SAP Analytics Cloud for every presentation, create the PowerPoint once and refresh only when needed. This approach is perfect for recurrent presentations.
  2. Efficient Data Management: Save different versions of your presentations to maintain a data history.

With SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint you can:

  • Seamlessly connect to an SAP Analytics Cloud tenant
  • Quickly find SAP Analytics Cloud stories and related widgets
  • Select a widget and filter its data, if necessary
  • Add the widget to the slides.
  • Refresh the widget's data any time on demand.

Note: The initial Version of SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint supports only Microsoft Office 365 Web Version and Support for the desktop app is planned for upcoming releases.

 

2. Configurations and Pre-requisites for Getting started:

You can download the add-in from the Microsoft Office Store. Please note that you need an SAP Analytics Cloud license to access your data with the add-in in Microsoft PowerPoint.

Deploy the add-in to Microsoft Office PowerPoint 365 web version.

2.1 Prerequisites:

SAP Analytics Cloud tenants you use with the add-in must have several trusted origins sites configured. To configure the trusted origins, sign-in to your tenant and choose System Administration -->App Integration --> Trusted Origins and add the following sites as trusted origins:

https://*.online.office.com 
https://*.online.office.de 
https://*.live.com 
https://*.sharepoint.com 
https://*.sap.com

2.2 Tenant Configuration:

SAP Analytics Cloud tenants can be hosted on different domains. You can use tenants with the standard domain hcs.cloud.sap  without any additional configuration.

Tenants of the other domains, for example, sapanalytics.cloud or hanacloudservices.cloud.sap, must be manually configured by following the below steps as an Microsoft365 administrator.

  1. Deploy the add-in as an administrator. For more information, please see 2.4 Deploy and publish the add-in as administrator section. In step 8 of the deployment, choose Just me and Deploy.
  2. Install the Microsoft PowerShell on your machine in case it is not yet installed. For more information, please see Microsoft Powershell at docs.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell.
  3. Install the Microsoft PowerShell module O365CentralizedAddInDeployment. For more information, please see O365CentralizedAddInDeployment at powershellgallery.com/packages/O365CentralizedAddInDeployment/
  4. Configure your SAP Analytics Cloud tenants. The following procedure shows the configuration for the two tenant examples https://mycompany-qm.us10.sapanalytics.cloud  and https://mycompany-test.eu10.hanacloudservices.cloud.sap 
  • Open the PowerShell and connect to your Office365 tenant by running the command Connect-OrganizationAddInService.
    A logon dialog appears where you log in with your Microsoft365 admin user.
  • Register all your tenants hosted on the domains sapanalytics.cloud and hanacloudservices.cloud.sap by running the command Set-OrganizationAddInOverrides:
  • Set-OrganizationAddInOverrides -ProductId 98d87237-524b-4198-8d3f-03367054552e -AppDomains "https://hcs.cloud.sap","https://mycompany-qm.us10.sapanalytics.cloud ","https://mycompany-test.eu10.hanacloudservices.cloud.sap "
  •  Add all your tenant URLs separated by a comma and enclosed by "" to the command. The tenants of type "https://mycompany-qm.us10.sapanalytics.cloud " and "https://mycompany-test.eu10.hanacloudservices.cloud.sap" must be adapted. The tenant "https://hcs.cloud.sap"doesn't need to be adapted, but it has to be part of the command.

Note that from time to time the PowerShell loses the connection to the Office365 admin center. Maybe you must reopen the PowerShell and reconnect to the admin center viaConnect-OrganizationAddInService.

After successful Set-OrganizationAddInOverrides command execution, you should get a result with the modified configuration.

    5. Deploy the add-in to your users.

After a successful tenant URL configuration, you can deploy the add-in to your users. Go to the Microsoft365 admin center and choose the add-in to add your users/groups (step 7 and 8 in the Section 2.4 Deploy and publish the add-in as administrator ).

 

2.3 Deploy the add-in as a user:

To Deploy the add-in as a user, please follow the below steps:

  1. Open the Microsoft 365 PowerPoint web version in a browser.
  2. Navigate to Home Tab--> Add- ins --> Select More Add-ins

   3. Choose "SAP Analytics Cloud" Add-in from the Admin Managed dialog or Store. 

   Click on Open in Powerpoint 

Now the Add-in is launched in Powerpoint, click on Accept and Continue

5. Now SAP Analytics Cloud add-in is available in your Microsoft PowerPoint Ribbon.

2.4 Deploy and publish the add-in as administrator: 

As an administrator, you can use Centralized Deployment in the Microsoft 365 admin center to publish the add-in to users and groups in your organization.

Please note that the Configurations for the add-in are a prerequisite for using the add-in. Refer 2.2 Tenant Configuration section. 

Before you start, please check if your organization and users meet the requirements to use centralized deployment. The admin and the users must have Exchange Online and active Exchange Online mailboxes, for example.

For more information, please see the Microsoft Office documentation at Determine if Centralized Deployment of add-ins works for your organization

 
  1. Open the Microsoft 365 Admin Center and sign in.

    You can open the admin center from Office 365. To do so, choose the App Launcher and Admin: 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
For more information about accessing and using the admin center, you can see the Microsoft Office documentation at About the Microsoft 365 admin center
 
1.Open the link admin.microsoft.com to access the admin center directly.
2.In the navigation menu, press Show all, then choose Settings Services and add-ins.

3.Choose Deploy Add-In at the top of the page.
4.Choose Next after reviewing the requirements.

5.Choose the option Choose from the Store.

6. Search for SAP Analytics Cloud for Powerpoint to find the add-in
7.Choose Add to publish the add-in to other users.
8.Specify the users to whom you want to deploy the add-in and choose Deploy.

The process may take a few minutes. Users might need to relaunch Office to see the add-in in the ribbon. No further client configuration is required.

For general information about deploying add-ins centralized, you can check the Microsoft Office documentation at Publish Office Add-ins using Centralized Deployment via the Office 365 admin center

 

3. SAP Analytics cloud, add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint Features:

3.1 Sign-in:

Once the Add-in is successfully deployed, SAP Analytics Cloud add-in will be added to the Ribbon of your Microsoft PowerPoint web version and will open with the sign-in page.

1. Click on Sign-in from the SAP Analytics Cloud Ribbon.
2. Enter the SAP Analytics Cloud URL Ex: https://mycompany-qm.us10.sapanalytics.cloud .

Note: “GO” button is enabled only if the URL is valid and meets all the technical requirements for the Add-in. you can also click on “Trouble signing in? “Option which will re directs to help page which provides more information about the requirements and configurations.

3. Click on “GO”
4. Sign-in Credentials are not required if single sign-on is enabled, or if you have accessed SAP Analytics Cloud through the same browser.
5. If Single Sign-on is not configured, A new window opens to sign in to your “SAP Analytics Cloud” Tenant.

 

6. Enter the SAP Analytics Cloud credentials and click on Logon.
7. After successful login, the add-in landing page is displayed with Explore and Favorite tabs.

3.2 Explore and Add a Widget:

Access your SAP Analytics Cloud stories and widgets through the Explore and Favorites sections.
 
   a.Recently Accessed Stories:
       •View recently accessed stories from SAP Analytics Cloud.
    b.Search for a Story:
       •Use the 'Search for a story' field to find specific stories.
    c.Favorite Stories:
      •You can Mark and Unmark the SAP Analytics Cloud Stories 
      •Browse all stories marked as Favorites in SAP Analytics Cloud under the Favorites tab.

Note:

If a story in the Recent Stories or Favorites or from a Search results page list is greyed out and you can’t select it. you will see an information icon explaining about the below reasons.

  1. SAP Analytics Cloud add-in supports only the Optimized stories.
  2. Optimized stories without scripting
  1. Choose a story from Recent Stories or Favorites or from a Search for a story” field. Ex: SAP_OAG_DSC_TSWSHIPMENT story is selected.
 
   2. All the Pages in the selected SAC story will be listed.
       Ex: Pages that are available in SAP_OAG_DSC_TSWSHIPMENT story are listed.
  3. Choose and expand any Page in the SAP Analytics Cloud story and all the available widgets in this story are listed.

   Ex: Expand “Supply Chain Performance Overview Page”. -à all the available widgets with the chart type icon list are displayed.

   4. Click on any available list of widgets. Ex: “Planned, Actual and Delta Transit time” widget.

Tip : you can re-size the add-in to see the complete chart information. 

 5. Click on Add widget to add this widget as image into the slide.

   

 Tip: you need to un-select any objects in the slide and then click on Add widget.

6. On Mouse over of the widget, you will notice an edit button which will enable you to interact and change the widget with different options “apply a filter, Drill up, Drill down …… “

7. SAP Analytics Cloud Add-in Saves the actual state of the widget as a Bookmark that you have modified and added into the slide.

8. On mouse over, you will be able to see the tooltip information of data and you can do a filter on data point selection.

 

 
9. You can add more widgets to the slide. For Ex: slide below now contains various types of SAP Analytics Cloud widgets.

 

3.3 Data Refresh in the Widgets:

You can refresh the widget's data whenever you need ensuring that your presentations always contain the most accurate and trusted data without the need to copy/paste the information from your SAP Analytics Cloud system again.

SAP Analytics Cloud Add-in does a data refresh of the actual state of the widget that you have modified and added into the slide.

Choose “RefreshData” tab from the ribbon and it will launch the Refresh Panel.  

Refresh page displays all slides only having the recently added Sap Analytics Cloud widgets. All the widgets are grouped by slide.

It displays the widget information with Name, Last Refresh time stamp and Title of the SAP analytics Cloud story widget.

  1. In the above example In Slide2, there are 7 SAP Analytics Cloud widgets are added as images.  You can choose a single widget or select All to refresh all widgets in this PowerPoint Presentation.
  2. Click on Refresh button to refresh the selected widgets.
  3. All the selected widgets will be refreshed with the latest data.

Note Only the data will be updated during a Data Refresh; any changes or modifications to the SAC widget will remain unaffected.

 

4. Sharing of PowerPoint Slide to different users:

Microsoft PowerPoint slides are usually shared to different users. Let’s how add-in works when the PowerPoint slides are shared.

  1. PowerPoint Shared with View Access:
    • When an admin creates a PowerPoint presentation, adds an SAC widget to a slide, and shares it with users who have view-only access, those users will only be able to see the SAC widgets as images since they do not have permission to install the Add-in.
  2. PowerPoint Shared with Edit Access:
    • When an admin creates a PowerPoint presentation, adds an SAC widget to a slide, and shares it with users who have edit access, those users can install the Add-in. Users with edit access and valid SAC credentials can refresh the SAC widgets in the presentation.

5. Supported Features and some restrictions in the initial release.

  1. SAP Analytics cloud add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint supports only Office 365 Web versions.
    • Microsoft 365 for Mac (Web)
    • Microsoft 365 for Windows (Web)
  2. Google Chrome and Microsoft Edge browsers
  3. Only supports Optimized SAP Analytics Cloud stories.
  4. Below list of features will not be available in add-in :
    • Data Analyzer
    • Digital Board room
    • Analytical application
    • Stories created in Classic mode. 
  5. The stories listed below are displayed in PowerPoint but cannot be used:
    • Stories that are created with Force Prompts
    • Stories with Scripting
    • Stories with Value Driver tree.
  6. Table widgets and Geo Maps.

6. Tips and workarounds:

  1. Press the SAP logon button twice to access the Add-in Panel.
  2. Drag and resize the Panel to fully view the widget's data and legends.
  3. Keep widget images at their original size, as refreshing updates content but not size.
  4. Data Refresh option: Only data will be updated, not the formatting; and any another widget modifications won’t be retained.
  5. Make sure nothing is selected on the slide before clicking the Add Widget button.

7. Get SAP Support

If you have further questions, please open an incident in the SAP Support Portal on the component LOD-ANA-OF-PPA. 

References: 

  1. https://appsource.microsoft.com/en-us/product/office/WA200007270?tab=Overview 
  2. https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD_PPT 
  3. https://me.sap.com/notes/3496609 
  4. https://me.sap.com/notes/3289328 
  5. https://me.sap.com/notes/3496603 
  6. https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD_PPT?locale=en-US

entry.title: SAP Sales and Service cloud (V1 and V2) and Third-Party Cookies Deprecation
entry.content:
We have created an SAP Note where we listed our solutions: SAP Note 3409306 - Removal of Third-Party Cookies in Google Chrome and Microsoft Edge Browser. Follow this note to stay up to date. 

Please check if you are affected by third party cookie deprecation and what are the possible mitigation

Use Cases

As part of SAP sales and service cloud solution we allow customer to embed external application(running on a different domain) inside an iFrame using a mashup or as an standard configurations. 

Different use cases where applications are embedded inside sap sales and service cloud can be :

  • SAP analytics cloud 
  • SAP S4 Hana applications (e.g Create/Display service order)
  • Applications running on BTP / Build apps
  • Knowledge based 
  • any other customer integration with their third party applications

Short term solution

Please refer SAP note 3409306 for details :

SAP Sales and service cloud V1 and V2 has already implemented the top level deprecation trial token (1PDT) . This will enable customers to continue using cross domain embed till Dec 2024 (as per issued timeline by google).

Permanent Solution

All embed application need to migrate to support CHIPs and storage access API . Details available as part of SAP Note 3409306.

In addition we may have to wait for further roll out from google as it has revised its strategy regarding the third-party cookie deprecation and will introduce a feature to allow users to choose how Google Chrome behaves. While the use of the Privacy-Sandbox features is still relevant, the timelines and potential impact might change. We will update the blog according to Google’s guidance as soon as we know more.

entry.title: New Line Character String Length Issue In Groovy
entry.content:
Dear Community Users,

 

I am working with a simple Groovy Script to calculate the length of an incoming string with New Line Characters.

 

The script gives different lengths when tested in SAP CI SAP Integration Suite and Groovy IDE. I tested this in the Postman script, giving the same result as the Groovy IDE. From the looks of it, maybe it is not taking the newline as 2 characters in CI whereas Groovy IDE and Postman take it as 2 characters

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;
import java.util.HashMap;
def Message processData(Message message) {

def body = message.getBody();

message.setHeader("Payload-Length", body.length());

 

I need to perform hashing of the payload by calculating its length as a pre-requisite and this is not allowing me to get the same result.

 

How can I resolve this issue? Please help.

 

Thanks,

Kartikay Tandon

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud - Get Value by Data Action
entry.content:
Hi @guenay,

As already indicated this does work with Advanced Formulas (the scripting part in Data Actions) and setting the proper scope to avoid booking to all member combinations. Depending on your use case (and flexibility needed), you can also leverage the Cross-Model Copy Step by using the same source and taget model as shown below:

As it is the same source and target model, all dimensions are mapped to their identical counter parts. The magic is then in the individual copy rules (select the individual dimension members in the screenshot above) - here for dimension "A":

Here are the details:
* By selecting Auto-Generation Strategy "No Auto-Creation" you can create manual mappings according to your valid combinations (e.g. from # to A1 .. A4)
* By selecting Completion Strategy "Do not copy remaining members" all missing rules are ignored and only the scope configured will be copied
* Now create an individual copy rule to transfer data from your Holder member to your valid combination

Once repeated for all relevant dimensions you should be all set. As configuration is in the copy rules, this is a rather static approach - you could combine with the newly introduced Auto-Generation Strategy based on dimension properties to manage via master data - coming QRC3.2024.

Hope this is an alternate solution to your problem!

Regards,
Thomas

entry.title: External number range for materials
entry.content:
I am working on a project on S4HANA 2023 SP01. My client has a requirement to have external number range assigned to a specific material type and it should be possible to enter the following numbers:

  • Only numbers - for example 1234;
  • Numbers and special symbols - for example 170.70
  • Alphabetic characters - for example ABCD

If we create a number range 0 - ZZZZ, it is possible to create materials with numbers and special symbols and alphabetic characters. However when we try to enter only numbers the system gives an error “With this material type, ext. material no. must not contain only numbers”.

It seems there is no standard way to do the above.

We have checked the user exists in enhancement MGA00002 and MGA00003 and it seems we cannot use these to achieve our goal - it is always needed to have letters as pre-fix or suffix.

Do you know if there is some other UE / enhancement that we can use to fulfil our requirement?

entry.title: Re: Capture fright G/l account in MIRO.
entry.content:
Spoiler
 

 

In our current process,
the Purchase team generates Purchase Orders (POs) for materials without including the estimated freight charges. Consequently, the actual freight charges, which are billed by the vendor in tax invoice often result in a discrepancy between the PO value and the vendor invoice.

To reconcile this difference, the Finance team records the freight amount in a General Ledger (GL) account during the MIRO transaction. However, this method does not allocate the freight cost directly to the material cost.

so could you help me to solve this situation it really appreciated to me.

entry.title: Discover AI Innovations: Beta Testing AI Applications in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408
entry.content:
At SAP, we're dedicated to delivering the best possible experience for our customers. That's why we're inviting you to participate in our Beta Testing Program, where you can provide valuable feedback on new features and help us refine them before their official release.

By taking part in beta testing, you'll have the opportunity to explore the latest functionality before it's available to the wider customer base, and your input can directly impact the quality and usability of the features.

With SAP's continuous innovation in AI technology, we are excited to announce that our upcoming 2408 release will introduce a wide array of AI-related applications available for Beta testing. This provides you with an early opportunity to experience and engage with the latest innovations firsthand.

Finance

AI-Assisted Configuration for US Tax Jurisdictions

Utilize AI for extracting key information from natural language sentences or given Excel files, and automatically configure jurisdiction-based tax settings. This streamlines tax configuration processes, reduces manual errors, and enhances operational efficiency through natural language interaction and a more intuitive user interface, leading to a modernized and cost-effective onboarding experience.

Click to view the demo video

AI-Assisted Error Resolution

AFC's AI-Assisted Error Resolution effectively interprets technical error messages and context to provide understandable explanations and step-by-step resolution guides, saving time and preventing delays during the financial close process. It also empowers end-users to independently mitigate common error situations, reducing pressure and effort while modernizing the error resolution experience.

Click to view the demo video

AI-Assisted Financial Business Insights

Utilize a "Virtual Analyst" to provide instant explanation and summarization of insights in natural language and automate the discovery of significant data in Review Booklets for Cost Center. With AI, analyze and highlight data dependencies and suggest actionable insights presented in intuitive visualizations and plain language, facilitating management reporting and commentary.

Click to view the demo video

AI-assisted Explanation of Fixed Asset Depreciation Keys

Sales

Assisted Object Creation of Customer Return

Assisted Object Creation of Custom Returns feature utilizes AI to assist in the creation of return orders. The intelligent User Interface provides recommendations for recurring input values, ranked by probability, allowing the user to easily select from the offered recommendations, thereby streamlining the input process.

Project Management

Resource Management: Intelligent Matching of Employees to Projects

The feature of advanced skill match assists in finding skilled employees for resource requests. It considers similar skills to those requested and maintained in the candidate's profile, and presents a matching candidate list to the resource manager. This streamlines the process of finding the right candidates for resource requests.

User Experience

AI-Assisted Smart Summarization

Get summarizations for your object pages using SAP Fiori elements apps to facilitate decision making. Translate the summarization into languages other than your default language, and use it as a starting point for follow-ups. Easily share the summarization in your preferred solution and enjoy beta testing at no additional license cost.

AI-Assisted Easy Filter

Users can input filter criteria in natural language to obtain filter results in SAP Fiori elements-based list report applications. Verify the applied filter values in your classical filter experience and refine the filters as needed. Utilize the filtering experience that best suits your needs.

For more information, see: Get Involved Early

Register and experience the latest AI features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and influence upcoming AI developments.

entry.title: Authorizations – Restrictions for Production Orders Based on Master Data
entry.content:
Hello,

There is requirement to restrict the access to BOMs, planning of materials and production orders for a portion of the portfolio within one plant for users. System is S/4 HANA public cloud.

We manage to set up restriction for BOMs with field BEGRU_STLAN  and to planning data with PP_MRP_CTRLR  (except for MD04 tree for which we got answer that it is not possible to restrict order tree within one plant).

We can make additional restriction according to order type with field AUFART_WERKS  and we can only create or see only orders of designated type.

We can still go to Create production order (CO01) app and create orders for materials with different MRP controllers with this order type, so order type is nice to have, but not solution.

What is needed, is to restrict opening or changing production orders according to eg. Production supervisor (MARC-FEVOR)  or maybe MRP controller related to material… or by some field that can be read from material, production version etc.

Do you maybe have some suggestion for this matter?

Thank you in advance,

Kristina.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: OData Query: Filter on Hierarchy node is not working filterable=false
entry.content:
Hello,

according to Parameter Handling Hierarchy  it is possible to Filter on Hierarchy nodes.

I tried the following

Hierarchy is active (i.e $select contains _NodeID): Subtree selection => The reply contains all nodes (including leaves) below the filtered node. It is normally not possible to filter the _NodeID property. This is possible however if the query has a hierarchy node variable that was modeled for the hierarchy.

I created an Bex Query for 0PROFIT_CTR with an Hierarchyvariable  + NodeVariable . I used the following URL:  (HIERARCHYVAR='HIERARCHY1',HIERNODEVAR='')/Results$select=0PROFIT_CTR_N&$filter=0PROFIT_CTR_N eq 'HIER_NODE:Europe'

The result: Error Message: BW_BICS_EQ/033 --> Filter on 0PROFIT_CTR_N not supported

i can see filterable=false in metadata for 0PROFIT_CTR_N

How can we resolve this?

Kind regards 

 

-

entry.title: Re: Financial Statements from SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi again,

T-code F.01 is OK.

Choose the tab in the middle:

 

Anyway, when you start the transaction it will start, as you see in your attached screenshot, with the Tab "Further Selections".


There are 3 tabs, now choose the one in the middle (unfortunately your screenshot is in Japanese Language which Í can't read), it's called "Special evaluations":



In the first field open for entries "Balance sheet type" you can choose 4 different methods:

If you put the cursor into the desired field and press F4 then you all the available options., see screenshot abobe.

If you press instead F1 in that field you get a help text will be dissplayed:

Now you can play around with the different Balances Sheet Types.

Probably Type 2 is the one for your problem, but also Typ 3 is helpul if you want only for P+L accounts the monthly figures.

If you log on into SAP by choosing "English" as langauge you will get the same screen as in my example. Then it would be easier understand for everybody.

Choose here EN when loggong on:

entry.title: Re: BTP FinOps: Keeping Track of your Credits
entry.content:
Hi @samiksarkar 

Yes, this is related to the SAC part: if SAC is not available the 'Technical Allocation Dashboard' will not work. For this reason we provide 2 separate end-user roles that you can assign to users: "~btprc.cpkg_access_role" and "~btprc.cpkg_sac_role". If SAC is not used you should only assign the first role, so that the UI menu options relating to SAC are not visible.

entry.title: Re: SAP Manufacturing Execution training
entry.content:
Hi,

I found that if I assign the script "Nonconformances at Current Operation" to the VARNISH step and "Nothing Nonconformances at Current Operation" to the CAB step, both go to the VARNISH step. Conversely, if I assign the script "Nonconformances at Current Operation" to the CAB step and "Nothing Nonconformances at Current Operation" to the VARNISH step, both go to the CAB step. This means that depending on the "FALSE" value, only the "Nonconformances at Current Operation" is taken into account.

How to access technical support?

Regards

entry.title: Re: Simplifying HTML5 Content Consumption Across Subaccounts
entry.content: @fabianorosa both provider and consumer subaccounts must be on same IDP. When the user is already authenticated in Work Zone, and accesses an application which is routed to the provider subaccount, the application can access the existing user session and authenticate.

entry.title: Does oData V4 under ECC 750 support Lambda Operators for "Member Access Expressions" ?
entry.content:
OData defines two operators "any" and "all" that evaluate a Boolean expression on a collection. They can work on either collection properties or collection of entities. 

Now I wanted to do the same stuff like here in the blog of @Andre_Fischer with Material (MaterialGeneral - MARA to MaterialSales - MVKE)

OData V4 code based implementation - Overview - SAP Community

I tried for example this oData Call:

/sap/opu/odata4/sap/z_material/default/sap/z_material/0001/MaterialGeneral?$filter=_Sales/any(d:d/Salesorganization eq '0100')

But then i get the following error in SAP Gateway, that it can not generate the open sql where clause:

In note 2305033 I find some infos, but it is not totally clear for me.

In debugging I think I found the soulution (only unary and binary nodes are supported and not member access which is the case in my example):

I set up navigation properties etc, all things like $expand, $select and so work quite well. 

Am I right that this is not supported under ECC 750 and maybe under S4/HANA it is?

If not can i do some special ABAP implementation in my data provider class? Or should I direct start filtering from the MaterialSales entity, but then I can also setup navigation to MaterialGeneral and expand it, but then the cardinality is not as I prefer, because MaterialGeneral should be once in the response and MaterialSales can have multiple cases (for every salesorganization): MaterialGeneral 1 ... many MaterialSales 

Thank you,

best regards, Raphael

entry.title: Re: Product group default to "2" when using unplanned ser...
entry.content:
Hi, 

Thanks for reaching out! 

In SAP S/4HANA, the product type group is determined by the material type of the material or service you are using. For lean services, when you are inputting unplanned services, the system defaults the product type group to ‘1’ for Material. This is standard system behavior and cannot be changed automatically to ‘2’. You would need to manually change this each time.

Kind Regards, 

Roxana

entry.title: Re: HANA Vector Engine and LangChain
entry.content:
Hi @martagolabek ,

Thanks for asking. The code fragments are indeed a bit oversimplified to focus on the main aspect.

The “ConversationalRetrievalChain” can maintain a chat history, which I intended not to use here. The simplest way would be to pass an empty array as chat history to the “invoke” call:

answer = qa_chain.invoke({"question": "How can I use CORS in CAP?" , "chat_history": []})

An alternative would be to pass an instance of “ConversationBufferMemory” to the “ConversationalRetrievalChain”:

memory = ConversationBufferMemory(memory_key="chat_history", output_key='answer', return_messages=True)

...

qa_chain = ConversationalRetrievalChain.from_llm(
    llm=gen_ai_hub.proxy.langchain.ChatOpenAI(proxy_model_name="gpt-4"),
    memory=memory,
    vectordb.as_retriever(search_kwargs={'k': 20}),
    combine_docs_chain_kwargs={'prompt': PROMPT})

I will adapt the blog content accordingly.

entry.title: How to correct invalid time dependent MD in SAP BW
entry.content:
Issue :

  • In 0MAT_PLANT incorrect data loaded for Time Dependent master data .        

        

Reason :

  • When loading master data if we select below mentioned checkbox then it will consider as new record and it will create one new wrong entry in Q table

         

Resolution :

  • Execute RSDMD_CHECKPRG_ALL program to correct "From Date" for errored record 
    • First execute RSDMD_CHECKPRG_ALL program in check mode so that you will come to know that how many records have issue in data as per below screen shot :

               

    • Once you get list of Material and Plant which is in error you will get list then execute the same program to repaire the data.
    • So once you executed above program in repaire mode then you will see "from date" is adjusted properly.

                 

  • Then create self loop transformation for 0MAT_PLANT to update releavent attributes.
    • Write a code in end routine ( ABAP / AMDP ) in newly created transformation in such away that whatever next future records is available we need to set same attributes for errored record. ( This may vary based on business )
    • So in this example i need to show in ZVSSTA_TD = Z1 & ZORDTO_TD = Y for errored record.
    • When i executed self loop DTP for newly created transformation i can able to see below output which we were expecting.

entry.title: S/4 HANA DATABASE - SYSTEM USER - Best practices says to deactivate SYSTEM ID
entry.content:
S/4 HANA DATABASE - SYSTEM USER - Best practices says to deactivate SYSTEM ID

Wanted to Understand how many application Owners after installing HANA DB using SYSTEM ID are deactivating it. Or is it being used as a shared account by Basis Team? IS it being logged and monitored if its active. 

As the most powerful database user, SYSTEM is not intended for use in production systems. Use it to create lesser privileged users for particular purposes and then deactivate it.

Deactivate the SYSTEM User | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: After archive & delete the PO, Need to archive materi...
entry.content:
Hi, 

Thanks for reaching out! 

Archiving and deleting Purchase Orders (POs) in SAP does not automatically archive or delete related Material Documents and Invoice Documents. These are separate entities and need to be handled individually.

If a PO is deleted, it means that the PO is flagged for deletion and it will not appear in your regular transaction records.  However, the associated Material Documents and Invoice Documents will not be deleted or archived automatically. They remain in the system until they are manually archived or deleted.

It’s important to note that archiving or deleting Material Documents and Invoice Documents should be done carefully, considering the potential impact on your data integrity and reporting. Before deciding to archive or delete these documents, you should consider whether these documents may still be needed for future reference, reporting, or auditing purposes

Kind Regards, 

Roxana
